title: Extension Functions

slides.title: The Locked Box 🔒
slides.content:
## Reaching the Unreachable

Imagine thoroughly using Kotlin's standard core `Int` class. You suddenly realize you desperately want a fundamentally custom `.isEven()` member function natively attached to every Integer logically.

But explicitly, you didn't legally write the `Int` class physically... JetBrains did. So how explicitly realistically do you dynamically mutate it?

slides.title: Extension Power 🔌
slides.content:
Kotlin remarkably radically lets you explicitly uniquely "glue" completely brand-new custom functions aggressively fundamentally onto structurally existing classes universally externally organically.

Just securely prefix your completely new function natively with the Class name and a dot `.`
